title: Hydrogeologist
description: **SLR is a global leader in sustainability solutions, working with clients to help make sustainability happen, within a collaborative, people\-first culture.** Due to continued growth, we are seeking an experienced **Senior / Associate Hydraulic \& Hydrogeology/Hydrology Consultant** (or candidates with 5\+ years’ experience) to join our established **Hydrogeology \& Hydrology team** in Ireland, delivering high\-profile water environment projects. Based in **Dublin (hybrid)** – with flexibility to be based in other Irish offices or remotely for the right candidate – the role will focus primarily on **hydrogeology and hydrology assessments and project delivery** , with additional exposure to **flood risk, drainage, and wider water environment disciplines** , as well as projects spanning renewables, infrastructure, power, industrial and built environment sectors. **The Role** **Role** You’ll support and lead the delivery of water environment projects from early planning through to detailed reporting, working closely with clients and multidisciplinary teams. The role includes: * Project and technical delivery across hydrogeology and hydrology projects * Preparation of Environmental Impact Assessment Reports (EIAR) – Water chapters * Management of water monitoring programmes and associated data analysis * Development of hydrogeological and hydrological Conceptual Site Models * Permitting and licensing support (including Discharge Licences and Industrial Emissions Licences) * Site work including supervision of drilling, groundwater monitoring and water sampling * Technical report writing and peer review * Client interaction and support with proposals and project delivery * Mentoring and supporting junior team members * A mix of desk\-based work and site visits across Ireland, with occasional travel You’ll collaborate with colleagues across Environmental and Earth Sciences, Planning, ESIA and Engineering, as well as SLR’s wider Water Environment group, including flood risk and drainage specialists, geomorphologists and geochemists. **Rewards \& Benefits** SLR’s ‘One Team’ culture is at the heart of our business, providing a collaborative and supportive environment for professional development. Through a structured career framework, regular performance reviews, and full support toward chartership, professional qualifications and licenses, we offer outstanding opportunities for long‑term career progression. **Our Flexible Benefits \& Reward Package Includes** * Competitive salary with discretionary bonus * Company pension * Private medical insurance * Life, income, and travel insurance * Salary sacrifice car lease and cycle‑to‑work schemes *We believe sustainable careers need balance, that’s why we offer:* * Flexible, hybrid and agile working * 25 days’ annual leave plus flexible public holidays * Buy or sell leave options * Core working hours * Cross‑border remote working and sabbatical opportunities **Family Life And Wellbeing Matter At SLR** We provide enhanced maternity, paternity and adoption leave, access to an Employee Assistance Programme, and specialist support for mental health, stress, and menopause \- supporting our people both professionally and personally. **About You** You’ll bring: * Solid to substantial experience in hydrogeology and/or hydrology, ideally within a consultancy or similar commercial environment * A degree in Geoscience, Hydrogeology, Hydrology, or a related discipline (postgraduate qualification advantageous) * Experience delivering EIAR Water chapters or equivalent environmental reporting * Experience in developing conceptual site models and managing field programmes * Knowledge of water permitting/licensing processes * Strong technical report writing and problem\-solving skills * Experience with GIS software (desirable) * Project management and stakeholder engagement experience * A full, clean driving licence (required for site work) **Culture \& Inclusion** Inclusion is fundamental to who we are. We are committed to equal opportunities and foster a workplace where everyone can thrive, supported by active Culture \& Inclusion Committees and Employee Networks. We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds and are a Disability Confident Committed employer. If you require any adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know and we will be happy to support you. If this role feels like a great fit for your experience and ambitions, we’d love to hear from you.

title: IRM Analyst
description: **Description** The Information Security Risk Analyst is the operational engine of the internal risk program. While the Senior IRM Analyst and Risk Director define the strategic roadmap, the Analyst ensures the daily execution of that strategy. They are responsible for the "production line" of risk assessment: taking raw signals from the business, processing them through the established methodology, and outputting actionable risk decisions (Remediation or Acceptance). The ultimate objective of this role is Reduction of Uncertainty. By managing the program effectively, the IRM Analyst ensures that MongoDB’s leadership has a clear, quantified view of the top risks facing the enterprise. They transform the Risk Register from a static spreadsheet into a dynamic governance tool that drives accountability. The IRM Analyst must not be afraid to be in the trenches with the Engineering and Product teams. They are the primary face of the "Risk Intake Process," guiding stakeholders through the methodology. They are the gatekeeper of quality, ensuring that no risk enters the register until it has been properly scoped and quantified. **Responsibilities** Risk Identification \& Assessment * Execute risk assessments under senior guidance \- perform scoping, inherent risk scoring, control assessment, and residual risk calculation using established methodology * Conduct risk identification intake, manage the flow of requests from Jira Service Desk and the Issue Intake Tracker, review incoming submissions against entry criteria, assign Risk IDs, and replicate validated risks into the Risk Register * Act as the Triage Officer for incoming risk submissions, determine whether submissions represent strategic risks, operational issues, or duplicates. Filter noise to focus the team on signals * Develop risk scenarios for in\-scope assets by working with asset owners and risk owners , identify threat communities, threat events, and impact categories * Draft Risk Assessment Memos that tell a cohesive story from risk statement to risk rating to actionable recommendation. Progressively build toward independently authored memos that require minimal review notes * Monitor and flag emerging risk signals , including AI\-related risks (model integrity, data poisoning, shadow AI, third\-party AI dependencies) , and escalate with documented analysis for integration into the risk framework Control Identification, Mapping \& Assessment * Identify and document controls that mitigate assessed risks , map controls to specific risk scenarios and applicable framework requirements (NIST SP 800\-53, ISO 27001, SOC 2\) * Assess the design adequacy of controls , evaluate whether each control is appropriately designed to address the risk it is mapped to, and document findings with supporting rationale * Assess the operating effectiveness of controls , collect and evaluate evidence to determine whether controls are functioning as designed over the assessment period, and document results * Document control gaps and support remediation tracking , maintain clear records of where controls are missing, partially effective, or require compensating controls. Track remediation progress * Maintain control\-to\-framework mappings to ensure risk assessment outputs directly support audit and certification evidence packages (FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, PCI\-DSS) Risk Categorization \& Governance * Apply the established risk taxonomy and categorization methodology consistently across all assessed risks * Process risk acceptance requests in Jira , validate completeness, ensure documented context and stakeholder sign\-off, confirm time\-bound conditions, and flag concerns to the Senior lead * Maintain the Risk Register, risk inventory, and supporting trackers with obsessive attention to data integrity, no missing dates, undefined owners, or stale entries. A Risk Register with governance gaps is a program failure Reporting \& Stakeholder Engagement * Contribute to KRI data collection and dashboard inputs , support accurate, timely reporting that feeds executive risk dashboards and governance forum materials * Engage directly with technical stakeholders (engineering, product, infrastructure teams) during risk assessments , ask informed questions, gather evidence, and document findings * Progressively build the technical fluency to lead stakeholder conversations independently , develop working proficiency in cloud\-native architectures, SaaS security models, and common technical controls (IAM, encryption, network segmentation, logging/monitoring) * Translate technical findings into clear, business\-relevant risk language in all written work products Policy, Process \& Governance Hygiene * Support drafting and maintaining risk procedures, guidelines, and assessment templates across the IRM program scope * Execute governance hygiene , data quality, tracker maintenance, workflow adherence, evidence organization, and documentation standards * Manage the risk assessment pipeline in Jira, create and maintain workflows, dashboards, and use JQL to track the assessment ticket lifecycle **Requirements** Experience \& Education * 3–5 years of experience in Information Security, Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC), or Enterprise Risk Management * Experience performing risk assessments — including risk identification, inherent/residual risk scoring, and documentation of findings * Experience identifying, documenting, and evaluating controls — including assessment of design adequacy and operating effectiveness * Strong working knowledge of NIST CSF, NIST SP 800\-30/39/53, and ISO/IEC 27005 — ability to use these frameworks as a library of controls and risk guidance * Advanced proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, complex formulas) for risk data analysis and reporting * Jira proficiency — managing projects, creating workflows and dashboards, and using JQL * Ability to write clear, concise, and defensible Risk Assessment Memos * Obsessive attention to detail regarding data integrity and documentation quality * Foundational understanding of cloud\-native architectures and common technical controls (IAM, encryption, logging/monitoring, network segmentation) — with a commitment to building deeper technical fluency * Awareness of AI risk concepts and willingness to develop expertise in emerging AI risk and regulatory landscape * A strong track record of collaborating effectively across teams and levels * Bachelor's degree in Cybersecurity, Information Systems, Business Administration, or a related field * Certifications: At least, one of the following certifications is required \- CRISC, CISM, CISSP, or CISA **About MongoDB** MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the data platform for the AI era, enabling builders to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified data platform, the most widely available, globally distributed data platform on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud\-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi\-cloud data platform and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. With offices worldwide and over 67,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI\-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software. Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership Commitment, guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB. To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys. Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB, and help us make an impact on the world! MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter. MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer. Req ID: 1273425625

title: IT Support Engineer
description: ICT Support Engineer \- Long\-Term Public Sector Contract Dublin 2 \| Fully Onsite Day Rate Contract \| Initial 24 Months \+ Strong Extension Potential Looking for a stable, long\-term contract opportunity with broad technical exposure in a public sector environment? We're recruiting an ICT Support Engineer to join a well\-established public sector organisation in Dublin city centre. This role offers the chance to work across end\-user support, Microsoft technologies, endpoint management, and day\-to\-day ICT operations in a structured and collaborative environment. With an initial 24\-month term and strong extension potential, this is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to build contract experience or move into a more stable technical support environment. The Role You'll provide hands\-on ICT support across desktop, endpoint, and user environments while helping to ensure reliable, secure, and responsive IT services. Key Responsibilities: \* Deliver 1st and 2nd line ICT support across hardware, software, and end\-user environments \* Support Microsoft 365 administration, including user management and troubleshooting \* Administer and support Active Directory / Entra ID environments \* Configure, deploy, and support desktops, laptops, and endpoint devices \* Support endpoint management and device life\-cycle activities using Intune and related tools \* Troubleshoot Windows desktop and operating system issues \* Assist with account provisioning, permissions, and access management \* Support networking and connectivity troubleshooting (LAN/WAN, WiFi, VPN) \* Support endpoint security, patching, and compliance activities \* Manage incidents and service requests through ticketing systems \* Escalate and coordinate issues with internal teams and third\-party suppliers \* Maintain technical documentation and contribute to service improvement initiatives Requirements: \* 2\+ years' experience in ICT Support, Desktop Support, Service Desk, or Infrastructure Support environments \* Strong Microsoft 365 administration and support experience \* Experience supporting Windows desktop environments \* Experience with Active Directory and/or Entra ID administration \* Exposure to Intune, endpoint management, or device deployment tools \* Experience with ticketing systems and SLA\-driven environments \* Good understanding of networking fundamentals and troubleshooting methodologies \* Strong customer service and communication skills Desirable: \* Exposure to Azure environments \* ITIL certification or service management exposure \* Public sector, education, or regulated environment experience \* Experience supporting security and patch management processes Why Apply? \* Long\-term contract with strong extension potential \* Highly sought after, stable public sector environment \* Broad exposure across Microsoft and end\-user technologies \* Opportunity to build technical and contract experience \* Dublin city centre location

title: IT Support Engineer L1/L2 - MSP
description: **Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer – MSP** **South County Dublin \| 5 days office\-based \| MSP experience required** A small, well\-established MSP in South County Dublin is hiring a Level 1 / Level 2 IT Support Engineer to join its service desk team. This role is for someone with around 3\-4\+ years’ IT support experience gained in an MSP environment. MSP experience is required, as you will be supporting multiple clients, managing ticket flow, switching between different environments, and troubleshooting at pace. This is a 5 days per week office\-based role. This is non\-negotiable. You must already be living in South County Dublin, and you must be fully legal to work in Ireland now. There is no visa sponsorship and no relocation support for this role. Most of the work will be service desk based, supporting clients remotely, but there may be occasional site visits when needed. This is not a field engineer role, but you will need to be presentable, client\-focused, and comfortable visiting client sites from time to time. **What you’ll be doing** * Managing and resolving support tickets across multiple clients * Troubleshooting Windows desktop, user, application and connectivity issues * Supporting Microsoft 365, Entra ID / Active Directory and user account issues * Helping users with MFA, Authenticator apps and access\-related problems * Basic network troubleshooting across TCP/IP, DNS and related issues * Supporting security tools and processes such as EDR, MDR and general cyber security hygiene * Communicating clearly with clients by phone, email and ticket updates * Escalating issues properly when needed * Carrying out occasional client site visits when required **What you’ll need** * Around **4\+ years’ IT support experience** * Previous **MSP experience** — this is required * Strong Level 1 support ability, with some Level 2 troubleshooting experience * Good Windows troubleshooting experience * Good understanding of TCP/IP, DNS and basic networking * Experience with Active Directory and/or Entra ID * Exposure to Microsoft 365 * Awareness of MFA, Authenticator apps, EDR/MDR and general cyber security basics * Strong written and verbal communication skills * A positive, can\-do attitude and willingness to keep learning * Good troubleshooting instincts and the ability to move quickly between issues * You must be living in **South County Dublin** * Access to your own car or motorbike would be very useful, but it is not essential This role would suit someone who enjoys the variety of MSP work, wants exposure to different clients and technologies, and is happy working in a small, flexible team. Salary €35,000 \- €39,000\. Apply with your CV if you experience as a Technical Support Engineer and your location fits the requirements.

title: Identity Security Engineer
description: **Your role** Reporting to the Senior Manager of Cyber Engineering \& Architecture, the Identity Security Engineer will be a core member of the engineering team, responsible for the governance and continuous improvement of the Aer Lingus’ identity security posture (which includes privileged access). This hands‑on role supports delivery of identity security standards, secure‑by‑design architectures, identity lifecycle management, access controls and processes. **Responsibilities** The role will work closely with cross\-functional teams in Cyber, technology and applications, to implement identity controls and process governance, automation for identity lifecycle processes, management and oversight of our identity security posture and the health of underlying identity platforms. Responsibilities to include: * Support ownership of identity \& privileged security policies, standards and architecture patterns across AD, Entra and IGA services. * Implement and maintain identity lifecycle workflows and governance processes (joiners/movers/leavers, access requests, access reviews) leveraging SailPoint. * Support implementation of a privileged access platform, and the development of privileged access controls as part of identity services, including governance and lifecycle processes. * Engineer continuous improvements of identity security controls, PAM and IAM lifecycle processes, enabling self\-service and scalable services through automation * Deliver identity posture monitoring, and ongoing improvements of identity security controls. * Provide SME support for identity\-related security incidents and investigations, contributing to containment and remediation activities. * Support the building of identity metrics and dashboards, for centralised oversight of identity security controls coverage, effectiveness and risks. * Collaborate with application and platform teams to embed secure\-by\-design identity patterns (authentication, authorisation, RBAC, least privilege). * Maintain documentation and blueprints for identity security standards and processes, including configuration management of the identity security platforms. * Contribute to cross‑domain security architecture reviews. * Partner with Cyber Defence ensuring appropriate identity telemetry is being monitored. * Support cyber alignment with compliance requirements \& regulations. * Provide direction and oversight to third party providers that are supporting and operating identity services and platforms. **Your Qualifications And Key Criteria** * Minimum of 10 years’ industry experience with at least 5 years in identity management or identity security engineering roles. * Hands‑on experience with Active Directory and/or Entra in an enterprise environment. * Hands‑on experience designing, implementing and governing identity lifecycle and access lifecycle processes using an IGA platform * Experience designing \& implementing privileged access management controls and processes * Experience working with stakeholders to implement access governance and least privilege controls. * Relevant certifications e.g., Microsoft identity/security, CISSP/CISM/CIAM/CRISC * Strong understanding of IAM principles including zero trust, least privilege, RBAC, access reviews/certifications, segregation of duties concepts and lifecycle governance. * Ability to define secure identity architecture patterns and translate them into practical standards and blueprints. * Ability to implement identity standards and controls consistently and document outcomes. * Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills for identity and access issues. * Good communication and collaboration skills across technology, cyber and business teams. * Engineering experience with Identity Protection, IAM, and governance e.g. SailPoint, CyberArk, BeyondTrust, MS Defender for Identity, Crowdstrike Identity, SilverFort * Scripting/automation exposure (e.g., PowerShell) to improve identity governance processes. * Experience with identity threat detection concepts and integration with SOC monitoring. * Experience with non‑human identity governance patterns and modern authentication protocols. **Division / Department** Digital \& Information \- IT Other

title: Information Technology Support Analyst
description: Our Dublin based client are looking for an **Information Technology Support Analyst** to join their team on a permanent basis You will have the opportunity to become part of an established and strong team and work for a company that is a market leader in their field There is an on site presence required for this role and the office is Dublin city centre based **Responsibilities:** * Provide technical support to end\-users and assist in resolving various IT issues in an effective and timely manner. * Learn to identify and resolve IT problems and issues as they arise * Troubleshoot hardware and software issues on site and via cloud services * Assist in the setup, configuration, and maintenance of end\-user devices, laptops and phones * Maintain a highly organised inventory of IT assets and resources (ITAM). * Assist in monitoring and tracking issues to prevent problems occurring a second or third time. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure escalations and tickets are registered and dealt with effectively. * Contribute to IT projects, including software deployments and hardware upgrades. * Escalation of technical issues to other team members when necessary and required **Skills and Requirements:** * Minimum of 2\-3 years of experience in a technical support role. * Experience with Active Directory, Azure AD, and managing security groups. * Ability to handle access requests and managing permissions. * Proven capabilities in troubleshooting Office 365 issues and software installations. * Proactive problem\-solving skills. * Have strong verbal and written communication skills. * Demonstrated teamwork skills, attention to detail, and a strong work ethic. * Enthusiasm for technology and a desire to learn and grow in the field. **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities

title: Infrastructure Engineer
description: Our client is seeking an experienced Infrastructure Engineer to join their IT Service Operations team. This hands\-on role is responsible for supporting and enhancing core infrastructure, Microsoft 365 services, network operations, and security platforms. The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering secure, reliable, and high\-performing IT services while contributing to infrastructure projects and continuous improvement initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Administer and support Microsoft 365, including Exchange Online, Entra ID, and Intune. * Manage Windows and Linux servers, virtualisation platforms, and cloud infrastructure. * Support networking and firewall technologies, including Cisco and Palo Alto solutions. * Troubleshoot complex infrastructure, network, and Microsoft 365 issues. * Deliver infrastructure projects from planning through implementation. * Coordinate with external service providers and support partners to ensure effective service delivery. * Maintain security controls through monitoring, vulnerability management, and patching. * Conduct Disaster Recovery (DR) testing and maintain technical documentation. * Contribute to ongoing service improvements and operational excellence. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Third\-level qualification in Information Technology or a related discipline. * Minimum 5 years' experience in infrastructure engineering, systems engineering, or IT operations. * Strong Microsoft 365 administration and systems engineering experience. * Good understanding of networking, firewall technologies, cloud platforms, and security best practices. * Experience working within a regulated environment would be advantageous. **Desirable Certifications** * Azure Administrator Associate (AZ\-104\) * Intune Administrator (MD\-102\) * Cisco CCNA * Palo Alto PCNSA * CompTIA Security\+ * ITIL 4 Foundation **Key Competencies** * Customer Relationship Management * Planning and Organisation * Problem Solving and Decision Making * Strong Communication and Interpersonal Skills * Collaboration and Teamwork * Drive for Results **How to Apply** To be considered for this opportunity, please submit your latest CV.

title: Investment Analyst
description: ***On behalf of an established Irish real estate investment and asset management business in Dublin, we are looking to hire an Investment Analyst.*** This is a fantastic opportunity for an ambitious, technically strong analyst to join a high\-quality team and develop their career at the heart of a growing real estate investment business. You will work closely with and learn directly from senior leadership across investment appraisal, portfolio analysis and day\-to\-day asset management. **The Role:** * Support financial modelling and investment appraisal on acquisitions, disposals and portfolio transactions * Assist with the analysis of asset performance, yields, returns and capital allocation across the portfolio * Prepare investment papers and presentations for board and senior stakeholders * Monitor market trends, comparable transactions and macro factors relevant to the sector * Work closely with asset management, finance and external advisors on live transactions **The Person:** * 1\-3 years of relevant experience in real estate investment, corporate finance, investment banking or a related field, including strong candidates from bank graduate programmes or property agencies * Strong financial modelling skills; DCF, IRR and investment appraisal experience is desirable * Real estate experience is preferred, though strong candidates from adjacent backgrounds will be considered * Currently pursuing or on a clear pathway to a professional qualification (ACA, CFA Chartered Surveyor, or similar) advantageous but not essential * Sharp analytical mind with strong attention to detail and good communication skills This is an excellent opportunity for an up\-and\-coming finance professional to take a meaningful step forward in their career within a well\-regarded real estateplatform. For more information, apply within or contact Conor Dignam at cdignam@alliedtalent.ie

title: Investment Associate
description: We are partnering with a leading European private equity firm on an investment associate search. This is an excellent opportunity to join a hands on investment platform, gaining exposure across the full deal lifecycle from evaluating and executing investments to supporting portfolio company growth, M\&A activity, and value creation initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Evaluate investment opportunities through market, industry, and commercial analysis. * Conduct financial modelling, valuation, and investment analysis. * Support deal execution, including due diligence, negotiations, and transaction structuring. * Partner with portfolio company management teams to drive growth and strategic initiatives. * Identify and assess acquisition opportunities across the portfolio. * Support value creation projects, M\&A activity, and exit processes. **Key Requirements** * 3–5 years' experience in private equity, investment banking, corporate finance, transaction services, strategy consulting, or corporate development. * Strong financial modelling, valuation, and analytical skills. * Excellent academic background. * Commercially astute with an entrepreneurial mindset. * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. * Proactive, intellectually curious, and comfortable taking ownership. * Passion for business, investing, and value creation. * Industry or operational experience is an advantage. *Due to the volume of applications, only candidates whose experience closely matches the requirements of the role will be contacted.*

title: Junior BCAR Coordinator
description: Stewart Construction, a top Irish Contractor with over 120 years of excellence in the industry, is currently seeking a proactive and organised **Junior BCAR Coordinator** to join our growing and supportive team. You'll work alongside experienced professionals on prestigious commercial, industrial, and educational projects. This is an exciting opportunity to grow your experience with a company known for delivering high\-quality work across Ireland. You will be working within a well\-established team on a variety of prestigious projects. The current workload is exciting and you will be involved at all stages. This is a great opportunity to be part of an expanding team. As a member of this team, you will have the opportunity to develop and bring added value to your career and gain additional skills with a BIM enabled contractor. **Key Responsibilities and Duties** * Carrying out quality inspections on all works with BCAR Manager/ Senior BCAR Co\-ordinator. * Collation of all project documentation relating to the assigned certifier/design certifier's inspection plans and the Building Control Amendment Regulations 2014\. * Liaising with the Assigned Certifier and Resident Engineers on NCR’s raised and review of close observations with them. * Co\-ordination of commencement notices * Co\-ordination of inspection plans and tracking of same during the course of the projects. * Compilation of required product certification from contractors with BCAR Manager/ Senior BCAR Co\-ordinator. * Tracking of open compliance issues on projects. * Co\-ordination and liaison with all parties involved in the construction projects. * Report weekly and monthly data and information to site teams, key stakeholders and partners. * Monitor BCAR Compliance, inspection status, quality performance and outstanding actions * Support project audits, reviews and compliance assessments as required. * Promote best practice in quality management, inspection processes and regulatory compliance. * Contribute to lessons learned initiatives and continuous improvement across the business. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of Building Regulations, BCAR requirements, industry standard and emerging best practices. * Miscellaneous duties and responsibilities as may reasonably be required by the Company from time to time. **Requirements:** * Minimum of 2 years’ experience with a Main Contractor (commercial project experience desirable). * Third level construction management, engineering, architectural, or surveying background. * Knowledge of Quality Control and Building Regulations in the construction industry. * Strong team member with the ability to identify and support implementation of quality improvements * Committed to Continued Professional development, willingness to learn, \& passionate about better building. * Good communication skills \- Must have the ability to communicate effectively. * Good administrative/organisational ability with high level of attention to detail. **Desirable:** * Valid Safepass and Manual Handling certification * Full Drivers licence

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: Due to continued growth, new contracts and an extensive pipeline of work, Ireland’s largest Civil Contractor have an exciting opportunity for **Junior** **Quantity Surveyors** to join their team working on a large\-scale civil infrastructure projects in **Dublin.** Candidates will be working on residential and large civil infrastructure projects. **Role:** These include but are not limited to: * Procurement, appointment and management of subcontractors \& suppliers through to final account * Produce accurate progress accounts * Preparation and assessment of interim and final valuations for progress payments * Monitor and report on project costs * Collaborate with project teams to identify and mitigate potential risks and cost overruns * Assist the pre\-construction team in the delivery of tenders * Stay updated on industry innovations, regulations etc **Requirements:** * Bachelor’s degree in Quantity Surveying. * 1 year experience in quantity surveying within the Civil Engineering sector or college placement/ internship * Strong knowledge of construction contracts, cost control, and procurement processes. * Proven experience with NEC contracts is essential. * Proficient in the use of COINS, Primavera P6, MS Project software and MS Office suite. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong communication and negotiation abilities. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. **Salary:** * €40K – €50K neg DOE * Company Vehicle/Vehicle Allowance * Benefits Package * Pension If you are a **Junior Quantity Surveyor** considering a career move, please feel to send your CV to **N.williams@gpcireland.com** or contact **Nathan Williams** on **0861502137** for a confidential discussion. Step into leadership, stay specialised in your field, or become an equity partner! **I don't have Recruitment Experience, can I still apply? Now you have the opportunity to become part of the legacy that has forever changed the world of beauty. Become a La Mer Expert and help bring this extraordinary skincare experience to our clients. **KNOW THE ROLE** * Deliver an outstanding, personalized customer experience (product application, demonstrations on hand and face etc.) * Recommend individualized skincare routines based on customers’ needs and lifestyle * Achieve sales targets and KPIs * Conduct product demonstrations and provide skincare consultations * Participate in brand trainings and educational programs * Represent the store’s exclusive appearance and the brand’s image * Support promotions, events, and product launches * Monitor stock and support the daily operations of the store **Know What We’re Looking For** * At least 1 years of retail experience * Excellent communication and relationship\-building skills * Experience in a luxury or cosmetic environment * Confident in building relationships with customers * Preferred, but not required: * Minimum 1\-year related beauty working experience. NVQ Level 2 * Experience with a cosmetic or skincare brand * Sales experience in a luxury retail environment **Know How We Work** **Experience is our business:** Our customer engagement sets the bar in service and experiential retail, giving our customers something to love every time. **We Are More Than Shops:** We create enjoyable places, to excite, where people socialise, discover, learn and are inspired, and where local communities are enriched. **We are Curators:** Our unique range of product and services are key to the sense of excitement that Brown Thomas Arnotts is renowned for, whether for a special day or every day, things that make our customer’s lives better. **We Care for Our People:** We believe a great customer experience starts with a great people experience. title: Laboratory Analyst in Forensic Science Ireland
description: **Job Title: Laboratory Analyst in Forensic Science Ireland** **Employer:** Forensic Science Ireland **County:** Celbridge, Co Kildare **Closing Date:** 3pm, Thursday, 2nd July 2026 **Starting Salary:** €42,098 **About the Employer:** Forensic Science Ireland (FSI) is an associated office of the Department of Justice, Home Affairs \& Migration and plays a pivotal role in Ireland’s criminal justice system. Its mission is to provide the Irish Justice System with a high\-quality integrated Forensic Service through independent, expert scientific analysis and advice to best international standards. FSI is based in a new purpose\-built facility at the Backweston Laboratory Campus, Celbridge, Co. Kildare. It currently has 210 staff, which includes scientists, Garda experts and analysts trained in forensic testing and reporting techniques, supported by administration and ICT professionals. FSI is expected to increase staffing and capacity in the coming years to support the growing demand for its services. Analytical staff work across three main disciplines: • Physical Analysis • Chemical Analysis and • DNA and Biological Analysis. FSI now has vacancies at entry level Laboratory Analyst. This is a great opportunity for scientific and technical professionals to become involved in a dynamic and growing professional STEM\-based organisation in a progressive field that is critical for Ireland’s criminal Justice system. **About the Role:** Reporting to a Senior Laboratory Analyst, Laboratory Analysts will be involved in delivering on a range of analytical techniques and generating reliable results on an on\-going basis. Laboratory Analysts at FSI play a pivotal role in ensuring that laboratory equipment and processes are operating to the necessary high standards for an ISO\-accredited organisation. Successful candidates will also help implement and validate new techniques to ensure that FSI is at the forefront of forensic technology and practice. The Principal Duties and Responsibilities of the Role will include some or all of the following: * Operating, calibrating and maintaining specific instruments and automated analytical equipment; * Performing routine analyses on drugs, body fluids or trace evidence; * Ensuring smooth running of the laboratory, e.g. stock control, room supplies, reagents etc; * Ensuring that all analyses are carried out in accordance with the Laboratory’s quality and safety policies; * Acquiring knowledge of underlying analytical principles and relevant legislative or other background information relevant to the task being performed; * Using the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) appropriately; * Maintaining records of analytical data and calculations and movements of exhibits and samples; * Participating in collaborative and validation studies and performing other non\- routine analyses under the supervision of scientists; * Reporting all analytical results to supervisory staff in an efficient and timely manner; and * Giving evidence in Court if required. Please note: The above is a general guide to the key duties and responsibilities of the role and is not an exhaustive description. Other duties and responsibilities appropriate to the role may also be assigned from time to time. Office Location and Vacancies Successful candidates will be based at Forensic Science Ireland, Backweston Laboratory Campus, Celbridge, Co. Kildare. Please note: Successful candidates may be placed in any of the areas of analysis/disciplines in FSI. There are currently a number of Laboratory Analyst vacancies. The Closing Date for this competition is Thursday, 2 July at 3pm. We are committed to a policy of equal opportunity and encourage applications under all nine grounds of the Employment Equality Act.

title: Loan Administrator
description: **Experienced Loan Administration Analyst** **The Role** Join our clients Loan Administration team to support the management of Commercial Real Estate, Shipping, and Aviation loans. You will play a vital role in handling daily cash reconciliations, processing transactions, and issuing key notices. Working closely with their Asset Management team, you will ensure their borrowers and lenders receive an exceptional, seamless service. **Core Responsibilities** * **Loan Managemen** t: Board new loans into our system, assist with drawdowns, and maintain accurate portfolio data * **Cash \& Transaction Managemen** t: Handle daily cash and position reconciliations, manage payments (creation, approval, and release), and report on transactions * **Communication \& Reportin** g: Produce and verify financial notices for borrowers/lenders, handle audit confirmations, and help build monthly performance metric packs * **System Administratio** n: Set up online mandate rights and maintain team access to client account **What We Are Looking For** * **Analytical Mindset** : Comfortable reviewing and understanding financial data and repor **ts.** * **Tech Savvy:** Strong IT skills, particularly **in Excel,** with the ability to handle complex calculations. * **Strong Communicator** : Clear, professional verbal and written communication skills for client and team interactions. * **Team Player:** Happy working collaboratively within a team while taking individual ownership of your workload. * **Proactive Attitude** : Driven to deliver excellent service and continuously improve processes.

title: Logistics Operative
description: Job Req Number: 109273 Time Type: Full Time At DSV we're building the world’s leading logistics and transport network. With this exciting growth comes opportunity \- and we’re looking for new talent to join us. Location: Ballycoolin, Dublin 15 Working Hours: Monday to Friday, 7am \- 3pm Salary: €32,000 per year We are looking for operatives to join our Specialist White Glove \& Technology logistics Team, where your top priorities will be safety, quality. In this role, you'll have the opportunity to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced time sensitive environment. **What Will You Be Doing?** * Load and unload high\-value, sensitive goods safely and efficiently * Unpack, position, and move items to designated locations * Deliver a white glove service standard, professional, clean, customer focused * Follow Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and work instructions meticulously. * Work collaboratively as part of a specialist team * Maintain clear communication with customers, team members, and management * Adhere strictly to all Health \& Safety procedures * Any other duties that may be assigned from time to time **About You** * Basic experience with hand and power tools * Physical fitness is essential for packing and loading responsibilities. * Strong teamwork and communication skills * Ambitious, well\-presented and professional **Desirable skills:** * PPT Licence (advantage but not necessary) * Safe pass (advantage but not necessary) * Possesses a good command of the English language (spoken, written, and reading skills). * Punctual, reliable, and flexible with working hours **What can we offer you?** Competitive Salary Package and an opportunity to be a part of and grow within a driven and successful company, with a team of 160,000, operating in over 90 countries. In addition, we can offer access to a selection of employee benefits, such as: * Enhanced Annual Leave Entitlement from your start, which increases with length of service. * Salary Sacrifice Cycle Scheme * Employee counselling, for support with physical and mental wellbeing * Company Pension, which, when a member of, also provides access to Life Cover and Critical Illness cover **Next Steps** If this sounds like the next step for you, please follow the links and apply with your CV without delay! Please be aware that all successful candidates will undergo necessary right\-to\-work checks and certain sites require 5\-years’ worth of referencing and background checks. We do not accept CV details from Recruitment Agencies unless DSV have engaged directly regarding the role requirements beforehand. **DSV – Global transport and logistics** Working at DSV means playing in a different league. As a global leader in transport and logistics, we have been on an extraordinary journey of growth. Let’s grow together as we continue to innovate, digitalise and build on our achievements. With close to 150,000 colleagues in over 90 countries, we work every day to offer solid services and meet our customers’ needs and help them achieve their goals. We know that the best way to achieve this is by bringing in new talent, fresh perspectives and ambitious individuals like you. At DSV, performance is in our DNA. We don’t just work – we aim to shape the future of logistics. This ambition fuels a dynamic environment built on collaboration with world\-class team players, accountability and action. We value inclusivity, embrace different cultures and respect the dignity and rights of every individual. If you want to make an impact, be trusted by customers and grow your career in a forward\-thinking company – this is the place to be. **Start here. Go anywhere** Visit dsv.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook.

title: Market Development Representative
description: **Company Overview** Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1\.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business\-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. title: Operations Summer Intern
description: ***About the Role*** As a Student Operations Intern, you will support the full lifecycle of a Greystar student community. This role blends on\-site resident hospitality with operational and commercial management, focusing on the unique needs of the student demographic and the critical Summer period. Please note that this is a 10\-week internship during June and August. ***Key Responsibilities*** * **Full Lifecycle Support:** Own the administrative journey from initial enquiry and room viewings to contract signing, guarantor verification, and the high\-intensity "Summer Turn" periods. * **Front\-of\-House Query Management:** Act as the first point of contact for all resident enquiries, resolving issues ranging from parcel collection and maintenance requests to account queries with speed and empathy. * **Leasing \& Compliance Admin:** Maintain accurate resident records within property management systems ensuring all tenancy documentation and right\-to\-rent checks meet compliance standards. * **Summer Short\-Stay Operations:** Support the transition to short\-term tenancies during the summer months, managing high\-frequency check\-ins for interns and summer school groups to maximize asset occupancy. * **Head Office Operational Support:** Bridge the gap between site and Head Office by assisting the Operations team with portfolio\-wide data entry, occupancy reporting, and process improvement projects. * **Operational \& Financial Admin:** Support lease processing, guarantor verification, and rent collection. Gain exposure to reporting, financial and compliance standards (Maintenance/Facilities/Health \& Safety) to ensure asset protection. ***About You*** * **The Student Expert:** Currently enrolled in a degree (Real Estate, Hospitality, or Business) with an interest in the Living sector. * **Agile Communicator:** Comfortable pivoting from supporting a student to presenting data to an Operations Manager * **Tech\-Fluent:** Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel is essential) and ready to learn specialised platforms like Yardi or Entrata ***About Greystar*** *Greystar is a leading, fully integrated real estate company offering expertise in investment management, development, and management of rental housing properties globally. The company's business model is unique in its ability to own, operate, and develop multifamily, student, and senior housing across the globe. Building a global platform with a local presence, Greystar continues to expand its geographic reach around the world, bringing a globally interconnected professional rental housing platform and industry funded with institutional capital to countries where the sector does not yet exist. Greystar’s pan – European platform was established in 2013 and has scaled rapidly, now with investment, operational and development presence in the UK, Ireland, France, the Netherlands, Germany, Austria, and Spain.* *One of Greystar’s core values is Equality, and as such, we continue to pride ourselves on being an equal opportunities employer, accounting for all protected and identifiable characteristics. We truly value diversity of thought, background and of experience. Please reach out to the Talent Acquisition team to explore opportunities at Greystar in more detail or visit our careers site at https://jobs.greystar.com/.*

Whether you’re honing your craft, expanding your skillset, or stepping into a leadership role, now is the time to join a team that’s both scrappy and hands\-on yet influential enough to drive change and shape product strategy. As part of the platform team you will be central in improving our customer experience across the business. **Key Responsibilities:** * Running discovery phases and helping define product requirements * Testing prototypes and hypotheses with users to collect feedback * Present your work to peers and leadership, async and in\-person * Take part in weekly design critiques, workshops, all hands, and articulation of ideas and design solutions * Expressing feedback with kindness and candor * Tolerance for ambiguity and healthy curiosity * Translation of quantitative \& qualitative insights into design decisions * Contribution to our culture: motivation, optimism, quality, integrity * Prior experience designing for consumer\-facing products * Visual design skills Join us in shaping the future title: QC BioAnalytical Sciences
description: **QC BioAnalytical Sciences** This job specification outlines the general responsibilities associated with the role of QC BioAnalytical Sciences \- Senior Associate on the Client Manufacturing site. This role will be responsible for leading, coordinating, facilitating, and undertaking activities pertaining to lab operations and compliance, with minimal supervision, acting as a role model for other lab colleagues. The role undertakes implementing continuous improvement projects and supporting a lean culture and Right First Time (RFT). The role also represents the QC unit by liaising with internal and external customers. Additionally, the role may involve carrying out additional work functions that are not described in this specification but are associated with their role. **Key Responsibilities:** * Experience in a wide variety of analytical techniques including but not limited to cell based\- Bioassays, Immunoassays, Cell Culture (use of cells to execute bioassays) and Aseptic Technique. Experience with liquid\-handling robots is also preferable. * Plan and perform multiple, complex routine/ non\-routine methods and procedures and a large variety of assays. * Participate in the peer review of analytical data. * Responsible for providing technical guidance and applying expertise and critical thinking to help to resolve technical issues. * Lead training of staff on technical aspects of job as required. * Develop, revise and implement procedures that comply with appropriate regulatory requirements. * Qualification of analytical equipment and related testing functions. * Participate in Analytical Method Transfers * Compliance with Standard Operating Procedures and Registered specifications. * Ensure the laboratory is operated in a safe manner * Maintain quality systems within the QC laboratories to ensure ongoing compliance to cGLP and cGMP. * Ensure timely completion of Laboratory Investigation Reports and deviations through the Non\-Conformance procedures. * Participate in the generation and update of SOP's, trend data, investigations, non\-conformances, validation protocols, reports, method validation/verifications and equipment qualifications. * Participate in regulatory agency inspections as required. * Plan and implement procedures and systems to maximise operating efficiency. * Manage and contribute to the achievements of department productivity and quality goals. Requirements * Bachelors degree in a science discipline * Microbiology degree acceptable, only if clear and direct experience with bioassays/immunoassays is present * Strong experience in Cell based potency bioassays min experience, Cell culture, GMP/GDP Elisa ( minimum 6 months, ideally over 1 year) * Hands\-on cell culture skills * Direct experience with immunoassay techniques, especially ELISA * Familiarity with GMP/GDP lab environments * Prior involvement in bioassays/immunoassays, not entry\-level (someone who can hit the ground running with minimal ramp\-up) * CVs should evidence routine lab testing, method execution, data analysis for the above * Experience in additional project\-based lab responsibilities (e.g., Lean/CI, error management, SOPs, method transfers) * Comfort with digital lab systems (e.g., Lens Smart Lab) * Experience with multi\-functional teams in a large QC setting
